name: Scottish Brown Ale
recipeInstructions:
1. Starting Specific Gravity: 1.047
2. Final Specific Gravity: 1.015
3. Alcohol by volume: 5%
4. Place Munich or Crystal Malt in a kitchen pan, cover with water, heat to approximately 66°C, and let stand for 45 minutes to 1 hour before starting.
5. Pour the grain into your boiling kettle (pot) using a colander, collecting the water below.
6. Rinse the grain with hot water (54-77°C) until clear runoff is obtained. Discard the grain; the liquid becomes part of the boil.
7. Dissolve Dry Malt, sugar (except priming sugar), Dextrin Powder, Gypsum, and Salt in at least two gallons of water.
8. Heat to a rolling boil and stir in Bittering Hops along with Chocolate Malt for 30 minutes more.
9. Add Aromatic Hops during the last two minutes of boiling.
10. Cool wort quickly to 70-85°C (21-27°C) before adding yeast.
11. Transfer cooled wort to sanitized glass jugs or fermentors, filling no more than 2/3 full.
12. Add yeast and attach an airlock; allow fermentation to proceed for 5-7 days until yeast activity ceases.
13. Siphon beer into a secondary container without disturbing sediment (repeat if needed).
14. Boil priming sugar and siphon into clean bottles, cap with headspace allowed.
15. Check after one week or two weeks; ales are most drinkable after three weeks.
MAKES: 5 US gallons
